source.title: Independent codes for spatial and episodic memory in hippocampal neuronal ensembles

confidence_rationale: High source quality (Science, Moser lab, well-replicated). Strong mapping fidelity for MECH-076 and MECH-077: the rate-remapping vs. global-remapping distinction directly maps onto REE's two modes of hippocampal geometry change (local deformation by residue accumulation = rate remapping; therapeutic context change = global remapping). Transfer risk moderate: spatial navigation, rodent CA3/CA1.
failure_signatures: Two separable remapping modes exist: rate remapping (field locations stable, rates change) vs. global remapping (field locations change entirely).
Rate remapping occurs for context changes within a familiar place; global remapping for place changes.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Hippocampal neuronal ensembles exhibit two independent remapping modes: rate remapping (firing rates of cells change but field locations are preserved, occurring when context within a place changes) and global remapping (field locations change entirely, occurring when place itself changes). These modes are separable and encode independent aspects of episodic memory.
mapping.ree_translation: The rate-remapping / global-remapping distinction maps directly onto REE's two modes of hippocampal geometry change. MECH-076 (residue as structural map deformation) corresponds to rate remapping: accumulated harm/benefit exposure changes the 'rate' (analogous to firing rate weight / attractor depth) of nodes in the viability map without changing their topological arrangement. MECH-077 (therapeutic remapping = therapeutic context change) requires global remapping: therapeutic intervention must provide a sufficiently novel context signal to trigger full reorganization of the hippocampal map rather than mere rate adjustment. This predicts a threshold phenomenon: small context changes produce rate remapping (residue modification within existing geometry) while large context changes (EMDR, context-reinstatement therapy, major life transition) produce global remapping (genuine geometric restructuring). The distinction is clinically significant: if therapy only achieves rate remapping, the traumatic trajectory nodes persist in the same topological relationship — they are just re-weighted.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The paper uses physical environments and place codes; the mapping to abstract z_world action-consequence geometry requires inference. Rate remapping and global remapping are defined for spatial contexts — their analogues in abstract state-space navigation have not been directly studied. The threshold between the two modes (what makes a context change 'global' vs. 'local') is not well-specified in the biological literature and may not translate directly.
mapping.source_context: Rodent CA3/CA1 tetrode recordings; within-day context manipulations (same room, different visual cues = rate remapping; different rooms = global remapping). Moser lab, Bergen.

confidence_components.notes: High source quality. Mapping to REE's residue geometry is conceptually strong but requires spatial→abstract inference. Rate/global distinction provides useful diagnostic prediction for therapy (MECH-077): threshold effect between context-within-map modification and full map reorganization.
